summary: Automatically dispatch the preview repository Pages deployment workflow after sync commit verification so users do not need to recreate or manually run site publishing.
bugClassification: Preview Pages publish not triggered by workflow-generated sync commits
rootCause: The preview sync workflow commits and pushes preview assets from GitHub Actions. Pushes performed by GitHub Actions tokens do not reliably trigger other push-based workflows, so the Pages publishing workflow could remain idle until manually recreated or run. The app workflow only verified/polled the public URL but did not explicitly trigger the Pages publish workflow after sync.
externalizationDecision: No HTML/source CSS change. The fix orchestrates preview sync and Pages publish workflows from the app notification workflow.

previewPublishOrchestration.tokenRequirement: PREVIEW_REPO_TOKEN requires preview repo Contents Read/Write and Actions Read/Write

workflowResult: pending-after-push; app workflow now waits for preview sync commit and dispatches the Pages workflow automatically
minimumUserAcceptance: After push, confirm Notify Hado Library Preview dispatches the preview Pages workflow and the live preview updates without manually recreating the site deployment.
